title: Frisket Preflight Report
description: Normalized JSON report written to stdout by frisket-preflight. The Qt plugin consumes this shape only — never backend-specific payloads.

schema_version.description: Report contract version. Version 1 required page and bbox on every finding. Version 2 adds explicit scope and optional location fields. Bump only with a coordinated engine + plugin release.

pass.description: True when errors[] is empty. Warnings alone do not fail the run.

profile.description: Profile name that produced this report (profile.name).

pdf.description: Optional path or basename of the checked PDF.

errors.description: Failing findings with severity error (or mapped to error).

fixups_available.description: Fixups the engine believes apply to this document given the profile. May be filtered dynamically by the engine; not necessarily identical to profile.fixups.

bbox.description: Axis-aligned box in PDF user space (points), page coordinates: [x0, y0, x1, y1] with x0 <= x1 and y0 <= y1. Origin is the page media-box lower-left (PDF convention). Present only when the engine has a meaningful page-space region to highlight.

object_id.description: Optional PDF object / resource identifier when known (stringified). Null or omit when not applicable.

type.description: Machine-readable finding type (kebab-case), often matching a check id.

message.description: Human-readable description for the report panel.

check_id.description: Optional profile check id that produced this finding.

scope.description: document: no page location; page: page-level condition; object: page object or region when bbox/object_id are known.

page.description: Required for page and object scope. Absent for document scope.

object_id.description: Optional PDF object / resource identifier when known. Never substitutes for scope.

type.description: Machine-readable finding type (kebab-case), often matching a check id.

message.description: Human-readable description for the report panel.

check_id.description: Optional profile check id that produced this finding.

safe.description: True only for fixups that do not change printed color/content in a material way. Most print fixups are safe: false.

params.description: Optional parameters for automated fixup invocation (e.g. mode for add-bleed).
